title: Oxalis rosei
taxonomy: Oxalis rosei is classified within the family Oxalidaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Oxalis, which consists of approximately 800 species. Oxalis rosei is a type of wood sorrel native to Mexico. The classification of Oxalis rosei is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Its classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida and the order Oxalidales.

More research is required to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Oxalis rosei.

intro: Oxalis rosei, also known as the pink wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Oxalis rosei include oxalic acid, flavonoids, and ascorbic acid, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Oxalis rosei include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe and calm skin irritations.
